name: Detection and Mitigation of Hallucination in Large Reasoning Models: A Mechanistic Perspective (Reasoning Score / RHD / GRPO-R)
authors: Zhongxiang Sun (first author) + 4 co-authors [uncertain on full list and institution]
dataset_size: N/A — method paper (introduces companion ReTruthQA evaluation set, size [uncertain])
ground_truth_source: N/A — method paper (uses ReTruthQA labels for evaluation)
domain: N/A — method paper (evaluated on Math, Science, MultiHopQA reasoning hallucinations)
error_taxonomy: N/A — method paper (identifies two reasoning-hallucination patterns: early-stage fluctuation in reasoning depth; incorrect backtracking to flawed prior steps)
license: N/A — method paper [uncertain on code license]
public_data_url: ReTruthQA dataset URL [uncertain — not surfaced in search]
signal_type: logit (late-layer logit-lens divergence) — projects late-layer hidden states to vocabulary space and measures divergence between successive late-layer projections
white_box_requirements: Hidden states from MULTIPLE late layers (not specified which exactly — typically last ~20%) AND access to the unembedding (lm_head) matrix to apply logit-lens projection. Requires forward-pass internals, so full white-box for non-quantised models. For AWQ/GPTQ quantised models the hidden states and lm_head are still accessible — method should be applicable to our 72B AWQ runs.
supervision: unsupervised — Reasoning Score is computed without labels; RHD detection is unsupervised at inference time [uncertain on whether a thresholding step uses any labels]
granularity: per-step (reasoning step) — distinguishes shallow pattern-matching from deep reasoning at the step level; supports trajectory-level aggregation
temporal_resolution: per-step / streaming-capable — Reasoning Score is computed at every step of the reasoning trace and used to flag early-stage fluctuation and incorrect backtracking; can in principle run during generation
trajectory_aware: true — explicitly models the trajectory of reasoning depth and detects backtracking-to-flawed-prior-steps patterns
streaming_capable: true [uncertain — paper analyses post-hoc trajectories but the underlying signal is per-step, so streaming use is feasible]
integration_mode: post-hoc detector (RHD) + training-time calibration (GRPO-R) — hybrid framework
spectral_signature_overlap: low-to-medium. The Reasoning Score is a logit-lens divergence (KL or similar) between successive late-layer projections. This is a logit-space signal, NOT an entropy-trajectory signal. They look at trajectory PATTERNS (fluctuation, backtracking) but in the time domain, with no spectral/Fourier/eigenvalue features. Some indirect overlap with our hypothesis: 'early-stage fluctuation in reasoning depth' resembles a spike-pattern observation. Does NOT compute eigenvalues, Dirichlet energy, STFT, or any explicit spectral features. Our spectral instantiation of similar trajectory-pattern intuitions remains novel. Overlap is medium on the high-level idea (reasoning trajectories carry hallucination signal) but low on the actual feature class (logit-lens vs spectral).
tested_models: Large Reasoning Models (LRMs) — likely DeepSeek-R1, QwQ, Marco-o1 family or similar [uncertain on exact list]
handles_long_context: true [uncertain] — reasoning models routinely produce long traces, so trajectory-level analysis implicitly handles long generations
reported_metric: AUROC / F1 / accuracy on ReTruthQA hallucination detection [uncertain on exact set]
reported_value: State-of-the-art on ReTruthQA across Math, Science, MultiHopQA domains [exact numbers uncertain]
overlapping_benchmark: GPQA-style science MCQ (similar to our Phase-8 GPQA Diamond setup); MultiHopQA (similar to our Phase-7 HotpotQA setup); Math (similar to MATH-500)
model_used_in_paper: Large Reasoning Models — DeepSeek-R1 family / QwQ-32B / similar [uncertain]
applicability: high — directly relevant to our AWQ/GPTQ quantised 70B+ pipeline. Logit-lens projections work on quantised hidden states; we have the infrastructure (Qwen2.5-72B-AWQ Phase-8). RHD is an obvious comparison baseline if we move to reasoning-model variants (e.g., DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-72B).
preemption_risk: MEDIUM-HIGH on the high-level claim (mechanistic / per-step reasoning hallucination detection in LRMs) but LOW on the spectral angle. The paper occupies the same problem niche we are entering with Phase-10 (agentic / step-level hallucination detection) and is published at a top venue (ICLR 2026). KEY DIFFERENCES we must articulate: (1) Their signal is logit-lens divergence between late-layer projections; ours is the entropy trajectory H(n). These are two complementary white-box signals. (2) Their framework is supervised at the threshold/training stage (GRPO-R uses RL with step-level rewards); ours is fully unsupervised (Nadler fusion of label-free spectral features). (3) They do not use spectral/frequency-domain features. (4) They test on reasoning models specifically (LRMs); we test on instruction-tuned non-reasoning models. CONCRETE DIFFERENTIATION: our pitch should be 'orthogonal entropy-trajectory signal that complements logit-lens-based RHD; potentially fuse the two at decision time'.
bracha_alignment: moderate — RHD provides per-step scores that are natural inputs for COIN/LTT calibration. GRPO-R is a training-time RL method (less direct fit for Bracha's calibration toolkit).
ofir_alignment: moderate — logit-lens divergence is a layerwise-feature signal that is a natural multi-view input alongside our entropy features (multi-view kernel / VSDE setup). The reasoning-trajectory framing also resonates with Ofir's spectral / state-space view.
notes: CRITICAL TO READ FULL PAPER. arXiv:2505.12886, ICLR-2026 published. Submission lineage: 2505 = May 2025 arXiv preprint, accepted at ICLR-2026 (April–May 2026). This is the closest competitor in the LRM-hallucination-detection space. Action items: (1) verify supervision level and training-data needs of RHD itself (vs GRPO-R); (2) check whether their Reasoning Score requires labels; (3) plan an ablation comparing entropy-trajectory features vs Reasoning-Score features on the same Qwen2.5-72B-AWQ Phase-8 GPQA traces.
public_code_url: [uncertain — no GitHub link surfaced in search]
gpu_requirements: Same as inference of the underlying LRM — paper validates on 7B–70B-class reasoning models, so single A100 80GB sufficient for ≤32B; 2x A100 or AWQ-quantised single-GPU for 70B [uncertain on exact setup]
min_samples_for_replication: ~100–500 reasoning traces should suffice for a directional comparison (consistent with our Phase-8 N≈198) [uncertain]
runtime_estimate: Reasoning Score requires forward-pass hidden states (single forward pass); marginal cost is one matrix-vector product per late layer to compute logit-lens projection. Negligible overhead vs base inference. GRPO-R training is RL — multi-day on multi-GPU setup [uncertain]
handles_step_localization: true — RHD explicitly localises failures (early-stage fluctuation, backtracking events) within the reasoning trajectory
cumulative_state_modeling: Per-step Reasoning Score profile aggregated into trajectory-level patterns. Specifically detects (a) early-stage fluctuation = high variance in score over early steps, and (b) incorrect backtracking = score drops at steps that revisit prior content. Closer to a pattern-matching aggregation than a single Φ statistic.
confidence_masking_addressed: Not directly addressed [uncertain] — paper focuses on reasoning-model-specific failure modes and does not engage with the Cheng et al. 2025 confidence-masking literature.
